rag.description: Use UpdateGmailTaskList to update an existing Google Tasks list during an automation run using either a direct OAuth access token or a stored credentials reference. It enforces critical checks like mandatory “Id”, supports optional Title updates, validates API success and required response fields, and stores the effective list id/title/link as Base64 Session parameters to keep downstream workflow steps reliable and repeatable.

qa.question: What is UpdateGmailTaskList in one sentence?
qa.answer: It updates an existing Google Tasks list for the authenticated user and stores the list id, title, and selfLink into Session parameters for later workflow steps.

qa.question: What key inputs and behaviors does UpdateGmailTaskList support?
qa.answer: You must provide Id to choose the list to update, you may provide Title to change the list title, and you can authenticate with either Token or Credentials, where Credentials takes priority and is exchanged for a fresh access token at runtime.

qa.question: How does this plugin fit into the workflow integration context?
qa.answer: It is an Action that runs as a task in sequential workflows, calls the Google Tasks API to update a list, and then exposes the updated result through Session output parameters for downstream rules.

qa.question: What are best practices for using UpdateGmailTaskList in automations?
qa.answer: Prefer Credentials to avoid copying raw tokens into workflows, ensure Id matches the intended list, and consume the saved Session parameters (Base64) in later steps instead of re-requesting list details.

qa.question: What rule/schema details matter when calling UpdateGmailTaskList?
qa.answer: Call it as an Action with pluginName “UpdateGmailTaskList” and an argument macro that supplies --Id and optionally --Title, plus either --Token or --Credentials; Id is mandatory and the plugin writes UpdateGmailTaskList:Id, UpdateGmailTaskList:Title, and UpdateGmailTaskList:SelfLink to Session parameters as Base64 strings.

qa.question: What is the manifestVersion for this plugin?
What is the plugin key in the manifest?
What is the pluginType for UpdateGmailTaskList?
Which platforms does UpdateGmailTaskList support?
qa.answer: Platforms is “Any”, meaning it is intended to run across supported environments without a platform-specific restriction.

qa.question: Which categories is UpdateGmailTaskList listed under?
Who is the author of this plugin?
qa.answer: The author name is Roei Sabag.

qa.question: What is the author link in the manifest?
What is the manifest summary for UpdateGmailTaskList?
qa.answer: It updates a Google Tasks list, supports Token or Credentials-based authentication with runtime token exchange, and saves the effective list id, title, and self link into Session parameters for reuse by later steps.

qa.question: Where is the official documentation for this plugin?
Where is the source code for this plugin?
qa.answer: The source code is available at https://github.com/g4-api/g4-plugins/blob/main/src/G4.Plugins.Google/Actions/UpdateGmailTaskList.cs

qa.question: How does UpdateGmailTaskList handle errors and failures?
qa.answer: If Id is missing, authentication fails, credentials lookup fails, token exchange fails, the API returns a non-success status, the response JSON is malformed, or required properties like id or selfLink are missing, the action fails and does not write the output Session parameters.

description: ### Purpose
The UpdateGmailTaskList plugin updates an existing Google Tasks list for the authenticated user using the Google Tasks API.
It allows workflows to change list metadata (such as the title) without manual edits in the Google interface.
It supports either a direct OAuth access token or a stored credentials reference that is exchanged for a fresh token at runtime.
After updating, it stores the effective list details as Base64-encoded session parameters for safe reuse in later steps.
### Key Features and Functionality
| Feature               | Description                                                                       |
| Update Task List      | Updates a specific Google Tasks list by its unique identifier.                    |
| Token Support         | Accepts a raw OAuth access token for direct API calls.                            |
| Credentials Reference | Uses a stored credential record to generate a fresh access token at runtime.      |
| Optional Title Update | Applies a new title when provided; otherwise retains the existing list title.     |
| Session Storage       | Stores the effective list id, title, and selfLink as Base64 session parameters.   |
### Usages in RPA
| Use Case                 | Description                                                          |
| Rename for Run Context   | Renames a list to include run metadata (date, ticket, environment).  |
| Workflow Organization    | Updates list titles to match dynamic workflow phases.                |
| Standardization          | Enforces naming conventions across automation-managed task lists.    |
| Multi-User Automation    | Updates user-specific task lists using stored credential references. |

edge_cases: Access token has insufficient scopes for updating task lists
Expired/invalid access token
Google Tasks API returns malformed JSON
Google Tasks API returns non-success status code
Id contains leading/trailing whitespace
Missing Id
Network timeout or connection failure to Google APIs
Response JSON missing required properties: id/selfLink
Title contains non-ASCII characters or emoji
Title is empty or whitespace-only
annotations.expected_result: The specified Google Tasks list is updated and its returned id, selfLink, and title are stored in Session parameters as Base64-encoded values.
annotations.notes: The action uses the list id, optional Title, and OAuth access token provided in the argument string to call the Google Tasks API and update the task list.

description: ### UpdateGmailTaskList: Update using access token
Update a Google Tasks list using a list id, an OAuth access token, and an optional new title.
The `argument` attribute contains literal values for `Id`, `Title`, and `Token`, and the action consumes those values when calling the Google Tasks API.
On success, the action Base64-encodes the returned `id`, `selfLink`, and `title` values and stores them in Session parameters.
The exact Session parameter names used for these stored values are implementation-defined.
If the API returns an error or the response is missing required properties, the action fails and does not store Session parameters.

edge_cases: Credentials id/name not found
Google OAuth token endpoint returns non-success status code
Google Tasks API returns malformed JSON
Google Tasks API returns non-success status code
Id contains leading/trailing whitespace
Missing Id
Network timeout or connection failure to Google APIs
Refresh-token exchange fails
Response JSON missing required properties: id/selfLink
Stored credential record missing refresh token
Title contains non-ASCII characters or emoji
Title is empty or whitespace-only
annotations.expected_result: The specified Google Tasks list is updated and its returned id, selfLink, and title are stored in Session parameters as Base64-encoded values.
annotations.notes: The action resolves a credential record by id or name, exchanges its refresh token for a new access token, and then calls the Google Tasks API to update the specified task list id with the provided Title value (if supplied).

description: ### UpdateGmailTaskList: Update using credentials reference
Update a Google Tasks list by referencing a stored credential record instead of passing a raw access token.
The `argument` attribute contains literal values for `Id`, `Title`, and `Credentials`, and the action consumes those values to resolve the credential record by id or name.
The action exchanges the stored refresh token for a new access token and then calls the Google Tasks API to update the task list.
On success, the action Base64-encodes the returned `id`, `selfLink`, and `title` values and stores them in Session parameters.
The exact Session parameter names used for these stored values are implementation-defined.
If credential resolution fails, the token exchange fails, or the API response is invalid, the action fails and does not store Session parameters.
Stores the Task List id returned by the Google Tasks API.
Identifies the exact list that was created, updated, or retrieved.
Enables later actions to reference the same list without calling the API again.
outputParameters.name: UpdateGmailTaskList:Id

description: Stores the effective title used after the list operation completes.
Reflects either the provided title or the generated default value.
Allows later steps to confirm or reuse the final list name without recalculating it.
outputParameters.name: UpdateGmailTaskList:Title

description: Stores the selfLink value returned by the Google Tasks API.
Provides the direct API URL for the specific task list resource.
Supports traceability and follow-up API calls using the exact resource link.
outputParameters.name: UpdateGmailTaskList:SelfLink

description: Identifies a stored credential record by id or name.
Triggers a runtime exchange to obtain a fresh OAuth access token before calling the Google Tasks API.
Simplifies authentication by reusing securely stored credentials instead of passing raw tokens in each request.
parameters.name: Credentials

description: Specifies the unique identifier of the Google Tasks list to update.
Targets the exact list that will be modified in the user account.
Ensures the correct resource is affected and prevents accidental updates to other lists.
parameters.name: Id

description: Specifies the new title to apply to the Google Tasks list.
Provides a clear name that helps users recognize and organize their task collections.
When omitted, the list title is not changed and the existing title remains in effect.
parameters.name: Title

description: Supplies an OAuth Bearer access token used to call the Google Tasks API.
Enables direct API access without relying on a stored credential record.
Is ignored when the Credentials parameter is provided, allowing stored credentials to take priority.
parameters.name: Token

summary: The UpdateGmailTaskList plugin updates an existing Google Tasks list for the authenticated user.
It supports using either a direct OAuth access token or a stored credentials reference that is exchanged for a fresh token at runtime.
It saves the effective list id, title, and self link into session parameters so later steps can reuse them.
